title: Mohnish Pabrai's Session at The University of Nebraska, Omaha on May 3, 2024
body: I think Li Lu already talked about your "not fully formed idea" in one of his Q&As I watched in Chinese. He said "value investing has two components: 1. buy at below intrinsic value, then wait for valuation to rise to intrinsic value 2. buy a good company at intrinsic value, then over the years the good management team will increase the intrinsic value for you". Basically, at any point of time, intrinsic value is a fixed number, but as years go by, the number can grow.
author: @BiologicalPump

title: Mohnish Pabrai's Session at The University of Nebraska, Omaha on May 3, 2024
body: It would be great if you would do a talk about ideas or investments that didn't work out for you. I believe that would also hold lot of insights for everybody to learn from.
author: @JayPatel-sp2pe

title: Mohnish Pabrai's Session at The University of Nebraska, Omaha on May 3, 2024
body:
Good morning, Mr. Pabrai, and I'd like to thank you for your incredibly valuable, educational, and entertaining videos, which I'm enjoying watching. I've ordered your book "The Dhandho Investor" , which I hope will arrive in the next few days. I have a question.
I see many investment firms are campaigning for acquisitions, especially in mathematics universities. Princeton for one.
Does this mean that having superior mathematical ability offers significant advantages to excel in the investment world ?
Thanks again, and best regards, Gino from Italy
author: @gino3286

title: Mohnish Pabrai's Session at The University of Nebraska, Omaha on May 3, 2024
body: if Berkshire wanted to expand See's Candy they should open a store in remote areas such as Nunavut or Easter Island and the store should open only one day a week.  This scarcity would make it a special event for people to meet with friends and family to purchase the candy.  The one day a week schedule would keep employee costs down.

author: @parzinirani7297

title: Mohnish Pabrai's Session at The University of Nebraska, Omaha on May 3, 2024
body:
@mohnishpabrai 53:10 Love all your interviews, Mr. Pabrai! Thank you for all that you do to share your philosophy. In the Q&A session here you mentioned that Chipotle had no clones yet. While that may be true in terms of size, I believe that Chipotle is actually a clone of a smaller chain Freebirds World Burrito.

I’d be fascinated to know if you think Chipotle actually benefitted from being a highly motivated (and likely well heeled) second mover rather than a true innovator.

Thank you!
author: @GneissSchistBro

body: The first 25-30 minute intro is very deep. Not sure many can appreciate it. Mohnish is incredibly generous as usual. Thank you.

title: Living in Omaha, Nebraska: The Biggest Pro & Con #movetoomaha #omaha
body: Lived in Nebraska my whole life. Its like that everywhere. Smaller the town the more inviting it gets. The way most of us see it is we all have ups and downs and we may not trust you with certain things or people, but youre just as human as I am. Wanna grab a beer? Or smoke a bowl? Lol.
author: @Garugontheblue

title: Living in Omaha, Nebraska: The Biggest Pro & Con #movetoomaha #omaha
body: What if someone doesn’t look like you? Are they still welcomed? Meaning people of color or darker hair and eyes.
author: @Kaia6485

title: Living in Omaha, Nebraska: The Biggest Pro & Con #movetoomaha #omaha
body: We relocated to Omaha for grandkids - yes, the people are one of the positives. Of the negatives - I was surprised that taxes were pretty high. Property taxes are higher than California. But they pay for the public schools - which overall are great.
author: @blorax5179

title: Living in Omaha, Nebraska: The Biggest Pro & Con #movetoomaha #omaha
body:
I definitely do not like the Children of the Corn cities and communities environments happening in America today. Im an older women looking for a more laid back country environment. Because I grew up in a city urban metropolitan environment.

So I'm looking for a small town feel without the small town cult environment. I heard most rural aeas usually have a outsider click mentally and Im not a socialist so Im not necessaril looking for friends. But Im definitely a very good neighbor Im very inviting, friendly and a great conversationalist ,welcoming , down to earth, cultured and a great person to hang out with. So the people always matters to me we don't have to be besties but its not good t live in a town of vipers in pre opposition and unwelcoming urban trolls.

So thats great to hear
author: @AprilCreary-r3u

title: Living in Omaha, Nebraska: The Biggest Pro & Con #movetoomaha #omaha
body: Honestly, it depends on where in Omaha.  There's random areas of omaha where people are just the worst types of humans for some reason.
author: @die20293

title: Living in Omaha, Nebraska: The Biggest Pro & Con #movetoomaha #omaha
body: You better take off the blinders, buddy.\n Feeling that way about people that you've met better hide your stuff that's of value cuz, they're looking to take it from you and you're an idiot, if you don't Omaha, should be listed as a crime capital.
author: @nuggethamlin6193

title: Living in Omaha, Nebraska: The Biggest Pro & Con #movetoomaha #omaha
body:
Is Omaha diverse?? How friendly are they to people of color..more specifically, black people???

My husband and I are thinking its time to get out of California..I really can't stand the people here anymore..completely unkind, selfish, rude and disrespectful..not to mention ridiculously overpriced and overcrowded..

I need some space and I need my faith in humanity restored...it would be so nice to have my kindness reciprocated and appreciated..rather than looked at as odd and therefore super sus...

But I'm mixed half black half white, my husband is black and I have 2 black sons...I dont want to create a more uncomfortable environment for us by trying to do the right thing.
author: @QueenWarrKings

title: Cruise ship passenger shows life in hantavirus quarantine
body: That's some really scary stuff and I'm sorry your going through all this. Hopefully they can make sure that you are safe to come out, just know that were all hoping for the best for you. Keep everyone updated daily, fingers crossed you make it through this all unscathed!!❤
author: @RestingWitchFace999

title: Cruise ship passenger shows life in hantavirus quarantine
body: Honestly if I am one of the passengers given the choice between quarantine at this hotel-looking unit specifically designed for quarantine and returning home to quarantine, I would choose the former without a moment of thought. It’s a very decent and comfortable setup with medical staff checking in every day. Even can have stuff sent into the facility to feel more at home is totally not what I was expecting. And it would prevent any chance of spreading and potentially killing their family as well as the general public. And if they really do ended up finding they have it, they wouldn’t risk misdiagnosis and would receive the best care almost immediately and that would greatly help their chances of survival.
author: @anyili3196

author: @DharmaDerelict

title: Cruise ship passenger shows life in hantavirus quarantine
body:
It’s so cool we can connect with the quarantine group and see their thoughts and how their experience is going.

I’m so sorry you’re going through this scary time. I’m so sorry for the one person who had tested positive and also sorry that your photo was used and people are assuming you are the one who tested positive. I saw your video on AP titled “cruise ship passenger shows life in quarantine and denies hantavirus infection”. These titles make it sound like the person in the photo had the virus and is denying being infected…. When in fact the person in the photo is a different person from the one who tested positive and this video is to clarify you were not the one who tested positive.

The last thing the world needs is sensationalizing a potential outbreak for clicks…. I am proof the sensational title scared me into clicking but dang people thinking you are that person then a title saying you’re denying it rather than clarifying is wilddddddd
author: @whoops8412
